Title: A maize epimerase modulates cell wall synthesis and glycosylation during stomatal morphogenesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase-associated stomatal signaling protein (PANGLOSS2)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N-glycosylated protein pool in maize leaf base/stomatal cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein glycosylation linked to signaling and development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its N-glycosylation was reduced/altered in bzu3 mutants, implying impaired stomatal signaling-related glycoprotein maturation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Glycoproteomics site identification; RPLC-MS/MS; site-directed mutagenesis (N554Q) and gel-shift assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAN2 was identified as a differentially glycosylated protein in bzu3 mutants. 2. Its N-glycosylation site was mapped to N554. 3. Mutating N554 altered electrophoretic mobility, validating PAN2 N-glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assessed in bzu3 mutant versus WT leaf base tissues
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAN2 is not the primary experimental focus gene; included because its glycosylation was directly validated as altered downstream of BZU3. The paper links altered PAN2 glycosylation to subsidiary-cell shape similarity between bzu3 and pan2.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N-glycosylated protein pool in maize leaf base/stomatal cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein glycosylation likely related to cell wall biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its N-glycosylation was reduced/altered in bzu3 mutants, suggesting impact on wall biosynthetic machinery
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Glycoproteomics site identification; RPLC-MS/MS; site-directed mutagenesis (N283Q) and gel-shift assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GT14 was identified among differentially glycosylated proteins in bzu3 mutants. 2. Its N-glycosylation site was mapped to N283. 3. N283Q mutation caused faster migration, validating N-glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assessed in bzu3 mutant versus WT leaf base tissues
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream validated glycoprotein, not a primary focal gene controlling the phenotype independently in this paper.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N-glycosylated protein pool in maize leaf base/stomatal cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protein glycosylation and fucose-containing glycan formation relevant to cell development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its N-glycosylation was reduced/altered in bzu3 mutants, consistent with disrupted glycoprotein modification pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Glycoproteomics site identification; RPLC-MS/MS; site-directed mutagenesis (N121Q) and gel-shift assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FUT was identified as a differentially glycosylated protein in bzu3 mutants. 2. Its N-glycosylation site was mapped to N121. 3. N121Q mutation altered gel mobility, validating N-glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assessed in bzu3 mutant versus WT leaf base tissues
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated downstream glycoprotein; not a central causal gene of the study independent of BZU3.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in epidermal cells early, then gradually enriched in guard cells and finally restricted to mature guard cells during stomatal development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all epidermal cells early; strongly increased from GMC division to stomatal elongation; restricted to guard cells at final maturation stage
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nucleotide-sugar interconversion linked to cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis and protein N-glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UGE/GALE family; NAD+-dependent epimerase/dehydratase fold; conserved catalytic triad S133/Y157/K161; NAD+ binding motifs GXXGXXG and YXXXK
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly epimerizes UDP-galactose and UDP-glucose, and also UDP-GalNAc and UDP-GlcNAc; reaction equilibrium favors UDP-Glc and UDP-GlcNAc (~3:1 over UDP-Gal and UDP-GalNAc)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cellulose; mixed-linkage glucan; UDP-GlcNAc-dependent N-glycosylation products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Guard cell walls, especially central canal/local thickening region; N-glycans on stomatal cell proteins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports guard cell wall thickening, dumbbell-shaped stomatal morphogenesis, and normal stomatal function; N-glycosylation supports protein folding/signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes UDP-Glc and UDP-GlcNAc homeostasis, enabling cellulose/MLG deposition, proper guard cell wall thickening, normal dumbbell-shaped guard cells, and normal stomatal responsiveness
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for UDP-Glc supply during maize guard cell wall formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; allelic mutants; CRISPR knockout; native-promoter complementation; stage-specific complementation; recombinant enzyme assays; UPLC-MS nucleotide sugar quantification; cellulose/MLG imaging; crystal structures; catalytic-site mutagenesis; exogenous UDP-Glc rescue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BZU3 was identified as Zm00001d029151 and loss of function caused collapsed, antenna-shaped guard cells and nonresponsive stomata. 2. BZU3 encodes a cytoplasmic UDP-glucose 4-epimerase that preferentially converts UDP-Gal/GalNAc to UDP-Glc/GlcNAc. 3. bzu3 mutants showed reduced cellular UDP-Glc, reduced cellulose and mixed-linkage glucan in guard cell walls, and defective local wall thickening. 4. BZU3 also influences UDP-GlcNAc supply and alters N-glycosylation patterns of many proteins related to cell wall synthesis and signaling. 5. Catalytic activity is essential, because Y157F abolished activity and failed to complement the mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light and CO2 shifts used to assay stomatal function; exogenous UDP-glucose treatment partially rescued guard cell shape
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Glycoproteomics, ProteomeXchange: PXD041642; structural data in PDB: 7XPP, 7XPO, 7XPQ
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although BZU3 could convert some UDP-Xyl to UDP-L-Ara in vitro, reverse conversion was negligible and this activity was not supported as central to the phenotype. Exogenous UDP-Glc only partially rescued the phenotype, supporting an additional role via glycosylation. Homozygous mutants were seedling lethal.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A target for engineering stomatal wall properties and grass stomatal efficiency, but strong loss of function causes lethality and severe stomatal defects; precise spatiotemporal or guard-cell-specific modulation would be preferable to constitutive disruption
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth defects and seedling lethality in homozygous mutants; stomata remain closed and nonresponsive, likely impairing gas exchange and growth
